titel: The Swedish National Audit Office's report on the state's efforts to ensure safe real estate transactions.
html:
The Swedish National Audit Agency (Riksrevisionen) has, in the report "A More Secure Housing Market – The State's Oversight of the Real Estate Agent Sector" (RiR 2025:35), presented its review of whether the state's efforts to provide buyers and sellers with adequate protection in real estate transactions are effective. This document outlines the government's assessment of the Swedish National Audit Agency's observations and recommendations.

The Swedish National Audit Agency assesses that the Real Estate Agent Inspectorate (Fastighetsmäklarinspektionen) works effectively with its oversight activities, but that there are shortcomings that affect consumer protection in real estate transactions. The Swedish National Audit Agency notes, among other things, that the Real Estate Agent Inspectorate's planning and selection of cases for review may pose a risk that the oversight does not sufficiently address serious problems, and that the Swedish Agency for Public Management's (Kammarkollegiet) oversight of the Real Estate Market Complaints Board is limited.

The government welcomes the Swedish National Audit Agency's review and largely agrees with the Swedish National Audit Agency's observations that the Real Estate Agent Inspectorate's oversight is generally effective, but that the oversight can be further developed. As the Swedish National Audit Agency notes, the Real Estate Agent Inspectorate's oversight has developed in line with the government's desire for a more proactive and risk-based approach. The government also agrees with the observation that consumers have good opportunities to report real estate agents and access alternative dispute resolution, but that this area can also be improved.

With this document, the government considers the Swedish National Audit Agency's report to be finalized.
tldr: The Swedish National Audit Agency has reviewed how the state protects individuals during the buying and selling of homes. They believe there are shortcomings in the oversight of real estate agents, which could affect the safety of private individuals.
lattlast:
The Swedish National Audit Office is an agency that ensures the state performs its duties correctly. In its report, it investigated whether the state is successful in protecting buyers and sellers in real estate transactions. It examined how the agency "Fastighetsmäklarinspektionen" (Real Estate Agent Inspectorate) works to ensure that real estate agents comply with the rules.

The results show that the agency does a good job, but that there are still problems. The National Audit Office believes that the controls are not always directed at the most serious errors. There are also shortcomings in how the bodies that handle complaints about real estate agents are monitored. This can mean that the protection for consumers is not as good as it should be.

The government has now read the report and largely agrees with the criticism. They believe that the control of real estate agents has improved and that they are working more proactively to identify risks. The government also sees that it is good to be able to report real estate agents, but they agree that there is room for improvement.

With this announcement, the government concludes its handling of the report. They look forward to continuing to develop the work so that real estate transactions become even safer for everyone who buys or sells a property.
